bug_report.summary: Right-click on rendered image/link no longer shows the link context menu (Open/Copy/Remove)
steps_to_reproduce: Open a markdown file containing a rendered image with a link URL (e.g., `![Alt](https://placehold.co/200x80/svg "He said \\\"hello\\\"")`).
In preview/rendering view, right-click the rendered image placeholder.
expected_behaviors: A context menu should appear with link actions (e.g., “Open link”, “Copy link”, “Remove link”), as in the before-change behavior.
actual_behaviors: No link context menu appears after the right-click in the after-change build (the menu shown before-change is missing).

bug_report.summary: Pasting Markdown after creating new file targets the wrong document (content inserted into Helpful Links instead of the new file)
steps_to_reproduce: Create a new Markdown file named `link-title-doublequote-whitespace.md` (File → New file… → Markdown → Save).
Click into the editor area intending to edit the new file.
expected_behaviors: The pasted Markdown should be inserted into `link-title-doublequote-whitespace.md` (the new file being created/edited).
actual_behaviors: After change, the paste is applied to the “Helpful Links” document (it becomes dirty and shows the inserted “Example link” content), indicating the wrong editor/document has focus.
